page: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The underlying hardware utilizes an AMD EPYC 9575F processor built on the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. This configuration includes 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor is equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average category. Memory performance presents a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 818.19 GB/sec, the PassMark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This memory limitation is also reflected in LLM inference benchmarks, where prompt processing for a medium 7B model is weak at 21.23 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 11.45 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the PREMIUM-4xCPU-8GB offers a compute-leaning profile with its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-core loads, which represents a key tradeoff for compute-intensive applications. However, the high single-core efficiency makes it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that do not continuously saturate all cores. This server is suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded data processing, Redis caching, and basic database operations that fit within the 8.0 GB memory limit.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it includes 100 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, particularly in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks. However, multi-core execution and memory performance, including PassMark memory metrics, fall into average and weak categories respectively. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a compute-oriented option, though the shared CPU model introduces potential resource contention. It is suited for workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching, and lightweight database operations that benefit from strong single-threaded performance but do not require dedicated CPU cores or massive memory capacity.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD. It features top-tier single-core performance alongside average multi-core and weak memory metrics.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 100 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-leaning virtual server combining top-tier single-core processing with local SSD storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Bundled 100 GB local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Shared CPU allocation and weak PassMark memory performance
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and lightweight databases
